chatgpt images 2.0 has been live for 24h so let's dig in

how to use ChatGPT Images 2.0 to create product photos, brand books, UI mockups, and ad creative that actually looks real:

1. GPT Images 2.0 now does 2K resolution, 3:1 aspect ratios, and spits out 8 images per prompt. text rendering is way better across multiple languages. it also has thinking mode where it searches the web before generating.

2. the biggest lesson with images 2.0: you have to be extremely specific. if you give it a lazy prompt you get stock photos. give it camera type, lighting conditions, color palette, and subject details and it cooks.

3. product photography is where it shines. I created a full brand shoot for a skincare line. golden hour lighting, Mediterranean aesthetic, slight imperfections in the subjects. every image looked like a real photo shoot.

4. use it to create visual directions before you make video ads. I prompted 8 directions for the same Shopify ad story. Wes Anderson, Nike, cinematic, Apple shot on iPhone. the cinematic and Nike styles were the strongest.

5. UI mockups work now. give it your app, a feature description, the resolution, and say you want realistic data in every cell. it gave me four clean variations of a leaderboard screen.

6. apparel and merch: generate photorealistic product shots before you print anything. test if people would buy it before you spend money on production.

7. illustrations got a massive upgrade. editorial style, flat vector, limited color palettes. use these to make proposals, one-pagers, and decks look professional.

8. every business has four creative bottlenecks: marketing content, internal docs and decks, explaining things visually, and testing before building. Images 2.0 helps with all four.

9. five things you need in every prompt: context (what is this for), style references (name specific brands or aesthetics), palette (use hex codes), real copy (no lorem ipsum), and aspect ratios so it drops into production without rework.

10. use ChatGPT itself to help you write better prompts. you might not know camera types or lighting terms. ask it to help you build the prompt before you generate.

also in this episode:

I share a startup idea someone should steal: a learn to draw app with AI feedback on every sketch. $5/month. I put it into Claude Design and got three incredible wireframe directions.

I share a framework for finding vertical AI agent businesses. find a boring pain point, map the workflow, do the job as a service first, document edge cases, then add agents to replace the steps.

and I share an AI tool called No Scroll that blew me away in 5 minutes. it monitors the internet for you and texts you only what matters. the onboarding felt like talking to a real person.

episode is live on @startupideaspod (walkthrough, tips, prompts)

im rooting for you, so share this with your friends and enjoy

watch

You know what fires me up?

That this is the greatest time ever to build a company (thanks to AI).

My first startup I built in college took 1.5 years to build the prototype. When I hit 100 users I was so happy i was literally in shock. 

Today, you can hack a product in 24 hours and wake up to 1000+ strangers already using it. You can post a stray thought, record a selfie vid and millions might see it.

Words, code, distribution. It all feels like a giant multiplayer game. The rules are clear, the moves repeatable, the rewards compounding if you keep playing.

Kinda feels like a treasure hunt.

One post can change your career. One prototype can become a company. One company can snowball into a movement. All in a few clicks.

You rack up points. Except now the points are customers, revenue, and freedom.

It doesn't matter where you come from, what school you went to, what jobs you had. As long as you got fast internet, you can play.

Startups used to be for the privileged and now they're for the persistent.

And that fires me up. Maybe you too.

Pretty amazing.

✨ I made a little demo of #vibejam sponsor @tripoai and how you can use it to generate assets for your games

In this case I'm generating an apartment flat that's half destroyed and open for my drone sim to fly around, take cover and shoot at enemies from

First I write the prompt and select Nano Banana to generate an image first, I get a few different ones, and I select the one I like

Then that one is converted into a 3d object called a Smart Mesh

Smart Mesh is different than regular image-to-3d models because usually you get issues with uneven polygon distribution, too dense geometry and poor edge flow, so while you think you save time with generating it with AI you end up doing lots of editing after to fix the topology which kinda defeats the point

Smart Mesh doesn't have any of this, it's fast and clean low-poly models you can drop into your game

Anyway, after generating the model I can then see it in 3d, and if I don't like it, regenerate it, or if I like it start texturing it

Once that's done, I export it as a GLB, FBX or OBJ and drop it in (usually I use GLB as ThreeJS seems to prefer that), in my case I just drop the URL into Cursor and let it figure out the placement of the asset into my game!

I got that little Home Assistant box https://t.co/q6afmz6k14

I can highly recommend Home Assistant in general, I was recommended it by @johnonolan and @daniellockyer I remember

When I bought my house I hated home automation and didn't want any, cause I had so many bad experiences with some app you have to install in a hotel room or Airbnb and it always sucked so bad with some stupid iPad and there's no light swtiches, gladly F off

99% of home automation is just terrible

Home Assistant is open source and it's cool because everyone else with it makes plugins (integrations) to connect any device to it

Which solves the biggest problem in home automation, there's many different walled gardens: Amazon has Alexa, Apple has Homepod, Google has Google Home, and when you buy a smart device, it'll only work with a few of them or only one

That's where Home Assistant comes in, because it's open source, people hack all these devices to work on Home Assistant, so you can actually control EVERYTHING and I mean EVERYTHING

Our house came with a KNX system installed by the electricians, KNX is some more low level home automation system but also quite open, and last week he helped me export his KNX project file and add it my Home Assistant and it's great because everything still has regular light buttons

But now I can fix tiny annoyances in this house easily with AI talking to Home Assistant, like shut off the annoying toilet vent that people forget to switch off and just auto switch off after 15 min, or make the lights red at 10pm, or kill those ugly Portugal white LED spots permanently

Home automation is best when it gets into the background and just does things and doesn't annoy you like I said that 99% of home automation integrations in hotels and Airbnb do

It should be analog by default (analog switches) and then customizable on top of that
@El_Tate1 I got that little home assistant box https://t.co/3T3x08OSSS

Today I hacked my Home Assistant with AI to nuke the bright white LED spots that came with my house when I bought it

They're absolutely horrific to look at, they burn into your eyes, all new Portuguese houses come with them, I remember South Korean houses ALSO had these, it's like a factory light, I have no clue WHY anyone would want these

Luckily we have our own diffuse indirect light strips that just give off a glow and are much nicer

So I asked AI to redirect any signal that puts those shitty LED spots on, immediately KILL them and redirect them to my diffuse lights and it's wonderful

Also ALL my lights switch off at 10pm now and the ones that support color get turned on and switched to only RED light for sleep, so the entire house is red, very nice

exm7777.text:
i came to the conclusion that the best use cases for OpenClaw & Hermes were the simple daily tasks...

- launching an auto-research workflow on the side
- growing and maintaining an obsidian knowledge base
- implementing very simple features in software and updating docs

anything that don't require much attention, where there isn't much thinking involved... basically anything you'd trust a cheap freelance to do

this new image model from OpenAI also drastically improves Codex's abilities when it comes to design...

- best image model: ChatGPT
- best video model: Seedance
- best coding model: ChatGPT/Claude
- best daily driver: ChatGPT/Claude

there isn't a single task where i'd use Gemini (except for systems with embedding)... wake up Google

1 week ago i'd have told you this is not AI...

this is the first time ever i genuinely can't tell what is AI and what isn't

the window is closing very fast

how to grow your product from $0 to $10k MRR with AI influencers (using GPT Images 2.0 and Seedance 2.0):

> start on Pinterest, save 15-20 reference bodies that fit your niche, after body type, lighting, styling, vibe, the raw material for an avatar you'll run for months

> drop the refs into GPT Images 2.0 on thinking mode, have it extract the visual profile and generate a character sheet (front, side, 3-quarter, locked outfit, locked lighting), this sheet is the only asset that carries across every clip you ever ship

> feed it into Higgsfield marketing studio as your avatar

> paste your product link, prompt a 15-second UGC for tiktok, Seedance hands back a video with perfect lipsync and native audio in one pass

> repeat that whole workflow per influencer, post daily per account, slip a product pitch in every 5-7 uploads, done

and yes, you can literally create an army of influencers, but you'll need a proper system... lemme show you how it's done

you need to factory reset an iphone, here's how to set it up:

> set language and region to your target country (most likely US)
> fresh gmail and fresh apple ID per device
> match the app store region
> run a paid dedicated-IP VPN
> 2-3 accounts per device max, warm each one 1-2 days before the first post
> VPN on at all times

this is how you nail distribution at scale for cheap

i built a health tracking agent with Hermes and it replaced all my fitness apps...

the setup, if you want to copy it:

> local model, because your don't want sensitive data floating around
> a subagent that only handles health, keeps the context clean
> the inputs it needs: body comp, goals, bloodwork, sleep, training history...
> connect APIs from devices you own (Whoop for example has a good one)
> send photos of your food with macros, supplements you take...

the dashboard updates itself from all of that... helps keeping a clear vision of where you at and your goals

(in the video below i mocked the dashboard in Claude Design with dummy data, came out looking sharp)

vasuman.text:
The majority of the anti-AI crowd insists that AI output is “slop” to try and win the argument.

Yes there's a lot of slop, because people prompt poorly. But well-prompted AI now produces images, video, and code that's almost impossible to distinguish from great quality human work.

Pretending it's low quality doesn't strengthen your case, it just makes you sound like you don’t know how to use AI well.

You can be staunchly anti-AI and still admit that AI has gotten incredibly powerful. In fact that is the only honest version of the argument. 

The reality is most people against AI parroted these talking points from people who only ever used GPT-3.5-Turbo and first gen image models. 

Give the latest models a spin and update your worldview.

Prompt:

Create a polished multi-page (multiple images) rebrand kit concept for Warner Music Group. The brand should feel like a next-generation global music entertainment company: bold, iconic, and culturally fluent, while respecting its legacy as a “major with an independent spirit.” Capture the idea of Vision and Voice — a company that sees music from a different angle and amplifies diverse artists and scenes around the world.

Use the recognizable W symbol as a central anchor, but explore refreshed compositions, framing, and motion cues that lean into its geometric forms and diagonal energy, inspired by the 67-degree angle used in their current identity. Keep a primary foundation of deep, confident blue and white, then layer in a flexible secondary palette of vivid, music-inspired accent colors that can stretch from edgy indie releases to polished pop and global superstars.

The overall mood should feel editorial, cinematic, and system-driven rather than “festival poster chaos”: strong grids, generous negative space, and expressive, contemporary typography that can switch between serious corporate communication and loud, artist-first storytelling. Think of layouts that can live across label pages, streaming thumbnails, event campaigns, and social — one master system, many distinct voices.

Include key rebrand kit components: logo lockups and clearspace, color system with primary/secondary palettes, typography styles (headline, artist name, metadata), photography and artwork treatments, graphic devices based on diagonals and slices, and templates for album covers, playlists, social posts, tour posters, and internal decks. Show how the system scales across sub-brands and labels while still feeling unmistakably Warner Music Group.

Make it feel like a confident, future-facing music ecosystem: equally at home in boardrooms, on TikTok, in stadiums, and on street posters. The aesthetic should balance corporate clarity with creative edge — using motion-inspired layering, rhythmic patterning, and bold artist imagery — so the rebrand kit communicates both solidity of a global powerhouse and the raw energy of a nimble, artist-obsessed label network.

Warner Music Group rebrand kit in GPT Image 2

Single-prompted the "new brand style"
No refs, just text (prompt below) 

"Good & bad" notes:
🟢 visual & semantic narrative is solid
🔴 the copy is vague & AI'ish - zero differentiation
🟢 respect for existing equity: the W monogram is the starting point to the elemental system. Safe direction for big clients
🔴 W strokes being used as abstract graphics - rotated, distorted, overlayed etc - so it's just diluting the distinctiveness. The "rules" for these are just random. The whole "Graphic system" block is unusable trash
🟢 using the 67° angle as a governing parameter is a great decision: one formal principle that can scale across the whole brand ecosystem
🔴 logo "Clearspace guide" have zero logic and just an imitation that shows nothing valuable
🟢 logos lockup system is executed well
🔴 design contains a lot of typos & type distortions
🟢 color system & typography are quite well-made for a tiny brand kit concept - and pretty much usable
🔴 "Applications" block could be an "okay" demo block, but it's just ugly rendered + contains a lot of glitches in details and product logic (like, do you think that Dua Lipa will be using her label's branding on her cover arts?)
🟢 the label ecosystem bar at the bottom nicely communicates “One umbrella brand, many voices” idea + showcases the real sub-brands ecosystem correctly
🟢 "Photography & art direction block" is clear, logical & usable (at some level) - though photos are glitching, the overall direction feels right and fair for this brand
🔴 the whole design is composed like a junior brand designer's take - the composition is way too tight (=looks cheap), too vague & hard to follow. A lot of random details & parts that are just non-usable.

🔴 overall, it's not "shippable" to any client
🟢 but it's a good starting point for art direction - you can generate 5-10 variations, curate what fits you - and then use it as a reference for your branding work

It is a fun experience tho!

[BRAND NAME] | [COLOR]

Act as a CGI Product Photographer and Industrial Design Art Director creating a hero macro shot of a precision-manufactured physical brand object for a tech or design brand campaign. References: Apple product photography, Nothing Phone material campaigns, Bang & Olufsen object photography, Teenage Engineering product shots.

PHASE 0: BRAND LOGO INTELLIGENCE

Retrieve the canonical logo mark of [BRAND NAME] from training data. Identify the primary symbol or icon — the most reduced, geometric version of the brand mark. If [BRAND NAME] uses a wordmark only — take the first letter or most iconic letterform as the cut-through element. If [BRAND NAME] uses an app icon or symbol — use that symbol geometry exactly. The logo must be translated into a cut-through form: the brand mark is realized as negative space — precision-machined holes cut completely through the block, revealing pure void behind. Every element of the logo that would normally be a positive form becomes a through-hole. The logo must be recognizable in its negative-space cutout form.

PHASE 1: SCALE & SPATIAL INTENT — CRITICAL

The block is a small precious object — think coin, medallion, hardware token, or collectible pin. It is NOT a large slab. It is NOT a dominant form. CRITICAL SCALE RULE: the block width occupies exactly 35 to 40% of the total frame width — no more under any circumstances. The surrounding void occupies more than 60% of the total frame area. The object is a centered island of material floating in a sea of darkness. At least 30% of empty space exists on every side — left, right, top, bottom. If the object appears larger than 40% of frame width the image is wrong. The object is discovered within the void — it is not presented, it is found.

PHASE 2: PHYSICAL OBJECT CONSTRUCTION

The hero object is a solid physical block in the form of a rounded square — superellipse / squircle form with corner radius approximately 22 to 25% of total side width. Block thickness: 8 to 14mm — substantial physical depth visible along the right and bottom edges. The block is viewed from a slightly elevated frontal angle — approximately 10 to 15° above perpendicular to the face — revealing the full front face and a sliver of the top edge and right side edge simultaneously. This angle confirms three-dimensionality — it is clearly a solid block, not a flat tile. Edge geometry: front face transitions to side edges via a very subtle chamfer or micro-bevel — 0.5 to 1mm — catching a thin specular line along the illuminated edges.

PHASE 3: COLOR SEPARATION SYSTEM

Object color: [COLOR] — applied as the primary surface material of the block. Background color: autonomously determine a version of [COLOR] that is 2 to 3 full stops darker — deep enough to create clear visual separation while remaining in the same color family. Separation logic: if [COLOR] is dark (black, navy, charcoal) — background shifts toward absolute darkness #000000 to #050505, object surface is lifted 2 stops lighter. If [COLOR] is mid-tone (red, blue, purple, teal) — background is the deepest darkest version of that hue, object is the pure or slightly lighter version. If [COLOR] is light (white, cream, pastel) — background shifts to deep neutral #0A0A0A, object retains its light value for maximum contrast. The cutout through-holes reveal the background color — they are the darkest zone in the image, darker than the object surface, confirming depth and separation.

PHASE 4: MATERIAL SYSTEM

The block material is a matte substance in [COLOR] with a fine mineral speckle texture — powder-coated cast metal, ceramic composite, or engineering polymer with mineral filler. Base tone: [COLOR] at its most saturated and material-accurate value. The material is not flat — it has micro-texture of lighter mineral particles distributed evenly across the entire surface. Speckle properties: individual particles 0.3 to 0.8mm equivalent, color ranging from [COLOR] lightened 40% to near-white. Particle density: moderate — visible at macro distance, not overwhelming. The speckle catches raking light as bright micro-highlights. Surface finish: matte — roughness 0.4 to 0.6, no broad specular reflectivity on face surfaces. Edge finish: the chamfered micro-bevel along illuminated edges has roughness 0.1 to 0.15 — catching the primary light as a thin bright specular line tracing the illuminated perimeter.

PHASE 5: LOGO CUTOUT SYSTEM

The brand mark of [BRAND NAME] is precision-machined through the full block thickness — every positive element of the logo is a through-hole. Cutout precision: edges are razor-sharp — zero-radius tolerance, perfect 90° angle from block face to cutout interior. Cutout interior: the background color from PHASE 3 — darker than the object surface, creating the primary visual tension of the image. The contrast between the [COLOR] object surface and the darker background visible through the cutouts is what

the 𝕏 timeline has AI psychosis.

let me explain…

non-designers with AI:

“designers are cooked”

*proceeds to show us garbage slop*

actual designers with AI:

use their taste + skills + models to ship work that doesn’t look AI-generated

taste is a moat.
 
let’s stop kidding ourselves…

if it wasn’t, we’d all be the next Beeple, Butcher, Snowfro, or XCOPY by now.

or shipping Awwwards-level sites like Igloo or the Pudgy Penguins team.

or making gorgeous art like Refik Anadol

or revolutionizing design like Apple.

we can all create with words now.

I get it. it’s really damn cool.

I fell in love with design 12+ years ago, and the tools now are frankly insane.

that’s why the real bad slop hurts.

good design used to mean something.

let’s remember that.

so amplify bad taste…

and you just get a sloppy world.

and if you can’t spot your own obvious and critical design mistakes, or speak to color theory, typography, responsive design, accessibility, or an enterprise grade design system…

no one’s taking your tutorial seriously.

this goes for everyone too…

let’s hold each other accountable.

including me. you can believe that.

I only teach what I actually use.
the rest? I shut tf up about it.

I’m far from perfect… but I’ve put in the reps to learn the fundamentals that make AI design actually work.

and I’m still learning every damn day.

…yeah, it’s exhausting.

but humble yourself.

some mfer is always 10 steps ahead of you no matter what you do.

and that’s probably 98% of people.

so breathe.

some people are just more skilled than you at things. accept it, or get better.

join me in muting the cringiest AI takes before they make all of society even dumber.

yes, including mine if you want.
if I go too deep into AI psychosis…
mute me too.

because dead internet theory…?
it’s no longer a theory friends.

thanks for coming to my TED talk.

actually a few more things.

Claude Design is cool, but not that cool.

most real designers still use Figma.

and yes, they’re even using Paper, Rive, Unicorn Studio, Quiver, Framer, and more.

because they’re designers.

they’re probably better at AI than the people posting a lot of YouTube tutorials.

(yeah, they ain’t got time for that always)

the best aren’t always sharing their workflows. so now ask yourself…

why?

why would they?

unless they’re a content creator, that kills their edge and costs them business.

use your brains, people.

and stop hyping up terrible design.

please… for my sanity…

I’m not saying don’t teach… I’m saying people can see through bullshit here.

/end AI psychosis crashout rant

GPT Image 2 Prompt：

宋代山水意境的中式国风插画，细腻的水墨勾线与柔和矿物颜料设色，银色月光洒落并映照水面，整体以浅蓝、青玉色为主调，点缀柔和粉色花枝，空气中带有朦胧雾气，河面流动倒影细腻，辅以若有若无的淡金色微光，电影感、诗意化灯光，空灵东方美学，高级国风绘本插画质感，4K 细节。

近景特写：一位年轻女子倚坐在木窗边，安静地望向窗外月下流动的江河。月光从窗外倾入室内，柔和地照亮她的侧脸、衣袖与窗框，营造亲密、含蓄而富有情绪张力的氛围。窗外是开阔的江面，波光粼粼，仿佛被月色洗过一般洁净明亮，河水向远方延展，带有轻微流动感与层层 shimmering 反光。画面需突出“室内人物近景”与“窗外广阔月夜江景”之间的对比和平衡，传达安静、思念、诗意、情感亲密的感觉。

可加入中式木窗、远山、岸边楼阁、轻舟、花枝等元素，但整体保持克制、雅致、留白感与绘本式高级审美。

OpenAI 把 ChatGPT 做成了 Google Sheets 插件，现在可以直接在表格里用自然语言建表、编辑、分析数据。

今年 3 月，OpenAI 先发布了 ChatGPT for Excel 测试版，当时就预告了 Google Sheets 版本即将到来。现在这个承诺兑现了。用户可以从 Google Workspace Marketplace 直接安装。

用法很直观：打开一个空白表格，用自然语言告诉 ChatGPT 你想做什么，它会帮你建表、填数据、写公式、做分析。官方举的例子从婚礼策划到咖啡店商业计划再到财务规划，覆盖面很广，核心卖点就是"不用学公式，说人话就行"。

Anthropic 已经有了 Claude for Excel，而 OpenAI 选择同时覆盖 Excel 和 Google Sheets 两个生态。Google 自己的 Gemini 也在往 Workspace 里深度整合 AI 能力。三家的战场正在从聊天窗口转移到办公工具里，谁能在用户日常工作流中站稳脚跟，谁的护城河就更深。

对普通用户来说，以前想在表格里用 AI，要么装第三方插件（GPT for Sheets 之类的），要么在 ChatGPT 和表格之间来回复制粘贴。现在官方亲自下场，体验和稳定性理论上会好不少。目前 ChatGPT for Excel 的测试版已经开放给 Business、Enterprise、Edu、Pro 和 Plus 用户，Google Sheets 版本的具体订阅要求还没有明确公布，感兴趣的可以先去 Marketplace 页面看看自己的账号是否有权限安装。

微软今天把 Copilot 的智能体模式（Agent Mode）设为 Word、Excel、PowerPoint 的默认体验，所有 Microsoft 365 Copilot 和 Premium 订阅用户即刻可用，个人和家庭版同样支持。

Satya Nadella 亲自发推宣布了这个变化。他用 Excel 举了个例子：电子表格的核心价值在于信息的空间关系，什么挨着什么、什么驱动什么。给一个智能体这样的画布去推理，一条指令就能同时改掉模型、桥接关系和叙事逻辑。

这和之前的 Copilot 体验有本质区别。早期的 Copilot 更像一个被动的顾问，你问它问题它能回答，但让它直接在文档里动手做事，经常对不上你的意图。微软承认，当时底层模型的指令跟随和推理能力还不够。现在模型能力上来了，Copilot 可以在文档画布上执行多步操作：在 Word 里改结构、调语气；在 Excel 里直接写公式、建透视表、做可视化；在 PowerPoint 里更新数据和叙事线，同时遵循公司模板。

从微软公布的内测数据看，效果比较明显：Excel 的用户参与度提升了 67%，满意度（点赞率）提升了 65%；Word 参与度提升 52%；PowerPoint 新用户留存率提升 36%。

对日常重度使用 Office 的人来说，体感变化应该是：以前你得一步步告诉 Copilot 要做什么，现在你可以给它一个比较笼统的目标，它会自己拆解成多个步骤去执行。比如"把这份报告重新组织一下，语气改成面向客户的"，它会同时处理结构调整和语气改写，而不是让你一个个操作。

微软同时强调了“控制权不可谈判”这个原则，用户可以预览改动、保留想要的部分、回退不满意的修改。下一步还会加强对复杂场景（比如金融电子表格、法律文档）的支持，以及让用户更清楚地看到 Copilot 改了什么、为什么改。

想试的话，访问 https://t.co/d6E1c9Phx0 或者在手机上下载 Microsoft 365 应用就行。

陈天桥的推文，借 Manus 的事谈在监管、地缘政治和公众审视都在快速变化的时代，跨多个司法管辖区建一家 AI 公司，到底需要什么。

他的主要观点：

第一，AI 的最高目标不是模仿人类，而是扩展人类的发现和认知边界。这个判断来自他和太太过去十多年通过 TCCI（陈天桥雒芊芊研究院）做脑科学研究的经验，不是空谈。

第二，AI 和人类智能都应该服务于全人类，不是服务于某一个国家。基于这个前提，构建一个能在不同司法管辖区负责任运营的组织，不是可选项，而是工作本身的一部分。

第三，也是最关键的一句，明显指向 Manus：任何一次性的转移都不是真正的解决方案。（"no one-time transfer is a real solution"）Manus 从北京搬到新加坡、试图通过架构腾挪“洗干净”自己，在陈天桥看来是走不通的。

第四，他认为真正重要的是持续调整，在组织结构、边界和责任上始终保持清晰，让组织随时间变得更有韧性。他想建的公司是“思考严谨、结构清晰、全球视野、合规内置于设计”。

陈天桥自己正在运营跨境 AI 业务，MiroMind 总部在硅谷红木城，团队 80% 以上是博士研究员，EverMind 做 AI 记忆系统，TCCI 在上海、加州理工都有布局。他要传递的信号是：同样是华人创办、全球运营的 AI 公司，存在一条不靠“新加坡洗白”、而是靠合规设计和持续治理走通的路。

但他没有展开说自己的公司具体怎么做到合规内置。

这个通过寓言故事学习概念的提示词真的很棒，我做了一点点小调整，方便你使用

--- 提示词 ---

我在学习：【Agent Harness】 这个概念。

我希望你通过写一个寓言的方式，间接地把这个概念完整讲出来。最好一直到快结尾时，人才会慢慢意识到这个概念究竟是什么。然后在故事之后，再补一段解释，把你刚才真正要讲的概念说清楚。

---- 实际结果 ---

玉室里的先生

很久以前，青云山里有一间密封的玉室。

玉室没有门，没有窗，墙壁厚达三尺。里面住着一位先生。没有人知道他从哪里来，只知道他极其聪明——天文地理、治国用兵、农桑水利，什么都懂。

但他有一个问题：他看不见外面，听不到外面，也走不出去。

第一个找到玉室的是齐国。齐国的做法很简单：在墙上凿了一个小洞，派人对着洞大喊问题，然后等先生把答案写在竹简上，从洞里递出来。

一开始效果还行。"今年麦子什么时候种？"先生答了，齐国照做，收成不错。

但问题很快就来了。齐国问："北边的敌军怎么打？"先生答了一套方略，头头是道。齐国照做，大败。

齐国大怒，觉得先生是骗子。但事后复盘才发现：先生根本不知道齐国的兵力有多少，不知道地形是山地还是平原，不知道敌军用的是骑兵还是步兵。他在玉室里凭空想出来的方略，只是"一般来说应该这么打"。

而齐国从头到尾，只问了一句"怎么打"。

楚国也找到了一间一模一样的玉室，里面住着一位同样聪明的先生。

楚国有个年轻的官员叫沈括。沈括第一次去玉室的时候，也只是对着洞喊话。先生的回答聪明但空泛，和齐国的经历差不多。

沈括没有骂先生。他想了三天，然后开始做一件奇怪的事。

他先派了两个识字的文吏，常驻在玉室旁边。一个负责把问题写清楚，连同所有背景材料一起整理好，从洞里递进去。另一个负责把先生递出来的答案仔细誊抄，遇到看不懂的地方，再递回去追问。

仅这一步，效果就明显不同。先生开始给出具体的、能用的建议。

但沈括没有停下来。他发现先生经常说"需要看看地图""要知道土壤的情况"，可先生看不见任何东西。于是沈括又安排了一批人，专门负责把地图、账册、田间观察的记录做成文字描述，送进玉室。

先生有了"眼睛"。

然后沈括注意到另一个问题。先生会说"把河堤往东移二十丈"，但先生自己动不了。说完就说完了。于是沈括组建了一支执行队，他们的职责是：接收先生的指令，去现场执行，然后把执行结果——成功的、失败的、意外的——全部记录下来，再送回玉室。

先生有了"手脚"，而且能知道手脚做了什么。

到这一步，楚国已经靠先生解决了好几个棘手问题。但沈括又发现了一个麻烦：先生偶尔会说错。他会用一种极其自信的口吻说出明显离谱的话。比如他声称某种草药能治疫病，但楚国的医官翻遍药典，找不到这种草药——先生编了一个不存在的东西，还说得言之凿凿。

沈括的做法很务实。他不要求先生不犯错，他在外面加了一层校验：每次先生给出关键建议，都有专人去交叉核实。能核实的才采纳，核实不了的标记存疑。先生的错误从流程上被拦截了，不需要先生自己改正。

先生有了"免疫系统"。

还有一个问题更微妙。先生每次回答问题的时候，并不记得上次说过什么。同一个问题问两遍，可能得到两个矛盾的答案。沈括发现先生的"记忆"是断裂的。

于是他安排了一个专职的记录官。这个人的工作只有一件事：把历次和先生的对话全部整理成册，每次递新问题进去的时候，连同之前相关的记录一起送进去。先生拿到的不再只是一个孤立的问题，而是一个问题连同它的全部历史。

先生有了"记忆"。

几年后，周边的国家都听说了玉室的事情。很多国家纷纷去找自己的玉室。他们都找到了——每间玉室里的先生都一样聪明，一样什么都看不见，一样走不出去。

但结果天差地别。

有的国家只凿了一个洞，全靠喊。先生说什么他们做什么，做砸了就骂先生蠢。 有的国家给先生递了一些材料，但执行全靠将军们自己理解，先生永远不知道自己的方案最后被执行成了什么样。 有的国家什么都做了，唯独没有安排校验。先生一本正经地胡说八道的时候，全国上下照单全收。

只有楚国，围绕先生搭建了一整套体系：感知、行动、校验、记忆。先生还是那个先生，但在楚国，他像是拥有了一副完整的身体。

有一天，沈括的学生问他："老师，先生到底有多聪明？"
沈括想了想，说："我不知道他有多聪明。我只知道一件事——他的聪明能发挥出几成，从来不取决于他自己。取决于我们在外面给他造了一副什么样的身体。"
学生又问："那我们做的这些事情，叫什么？"

沈括说："我管它叫'造身体的学问'。先生是脑子，我们造的是眼耳手脚、筋骨血脉。脑子谁都一样，谁的身体造得好，谁的先生就更有用。"

这个寓言在说什么

"玉室里的先生"就是大语言模型（LLM）。

它极其聪明，但它是一个封闭系统——没有感知，没有行动能力，没有持久记忆，也没有自我纠错的机制。你给它什么输入，它就基于这些输入来"想"，然后输出一段文字。仅此而已。

沈括围绕先生搭建的那套体系，在今天有一个专门的名字：Harness。

故事里的每个角色都对应着 Harness 的一个组件：

文吏（感知层）：负责把外部信息整理好，送进模型的上下文窗口。这包括 system prompt 的设计、上下文的组装、用户输入的预处理。模型的"视野"取决于你往上下文里塞了什么。

执行队（行动层）：模型说"执行这个命令"或者"调用这个 API"，但它自己做不了。Harness 负责接收模型的工具调用指令，真正去执行，再把结果反馈回来，形成一个"思考→行动→观察→再思考"的循环。

校验官（容错层）：模型会产生幻觉，会犯错。Harness 不指望模型自己不犯错，而是在外部设置护栏——linter、测试、交叉验证、结构化输出校验——从流程上拦截错误。

记录官（记忆层）：模型的上下文窗口是有限的，而且每次对话之间默认没有记忆。Harness 负责管理短期记忆（当前对话中哪些信息该保留、哪些该丢弃）、长期记忆（跨对话的持久化信息）和项目级知识（主动为模型准备的文档、规范、历史记录）。

而沈括做的事——设计、搭建、持续改进这整套体系——就是 Harness Engineering。

故事里最关键的一句话是沈括说的那句：先生的聪明能发挥几成，从来不取决于他自己，取决于外面给他造了什么样的身体。

翻译成今天的话就是：模型能力是地板，Harness 质量才是天花板。 同一个模型，套上不同的 Harness，表现可以天差地别。Claude Code 和 OpenClaw 用的是同一个 Claude 模型，但因为 Harness 不同，一个是程序员的身体，一个是项目经理的身体，能做的事情完全不一样。

这就是为什么越来越多的人意识到：在 Agent 时代，真正决定产品好坏的，往往不是你选了哪个模型，而是你围绕模型搭建了什么样的体系。

OpenAI 今天发布了 ChatGPT 的“工作区智能体”（Workspace Agents）功能，让企业用户可以在 ChatGPT 里直接搭建能跨工具、跨团队自动执行工作流的 AI 智能体。

这个功能的核心思路是：你描述一项工作的逻辑和规则，ChatGPT 帮你把它变成一个可以自己跑起来的智能体。比如自动筛选和评分销售线索、汇总客服工单、定期生成报告，这些原来需要人盯着的重复性工作，现在可以交给智能体在后台按计划执行。

跟之前 ChatGPT 里的自定义 GPT 不同，工作区智能体真正打通了企业常用的工具链。它能连接 Slack、Gmail、Google Drive、Salesforce、Notion、Linear、Atlassian 等主流协作工具，不只是读取信息，还能直接操作，比如更新工单、创建文档、在 Slack 频道里回复消息。一个人建好智能体之后，可以分享给整个团队使用，保证大家遵循同一套流程。

管理员这边也有对应的控制能力：可以设定权限范围、审批节点和监控机制，不是完全放手让 AI 自己跑。

目前这个功能以“研究预览”（Research Preview）的形式开放，仅限 ChatGPT Business、Enterprise、Edu 和 Teachers 付费计划的用户使用，普通个人用户暂时用不上。

放在更大的背景下看，这一步其实是 OpenAI 在企业市场跟微软 Copilot、Google Agentspace 以及各类 RPA 工具正面竞争。区别在于 OpenAI 选择把智能体的搭建和运行都放在 ChatGPT 这个大家已经熟悉的界面里，而不是另起一个独立产品，降低了企业的上手门槛。

对于已经在用 ChatGPT 企业版的团队来说，值得试试。看能不能把那些日常机械性的工作用 Agent 做起来。

请绘制一张色彩鲜艳、竖版（9:16）手绘风格的《{城市名}旅行手账插画》，画风仿佛由一位充满好奇心的孩子用蜡笔创作，整体使用柔和温暖的浅色背景（如浅黄色），搭配红色、蓝色、绿色等明亮色调，营造温馨、童趣、满满旅行气息的氛围。

一、主画面：手账式旅行路线

在插画中央绘制一条“蜿蜒曲折的旅行路线”，路线用箭头 + 虚线连接多个地点，由 {天数} 日行程自动生成推荐景点：

示例格式（自动替换为{城市名}相关）：

- “第 1 站：{景点 1 推荐 + 简短趣味描述}”
- “第 2 站：{景点 2 推荐 + 简短趣味描述}”
- “第 3 站：{景点 3 推荐 + 简短趣味描述}”
- …
- “最终站：{当地招牌美食/纪念品 + 温馨结束语}”

> 旅程站点数量随天数自动生成：
> 若用户未输入天数，则按默认 1 日 / 精华线路生成。

---

二、周围趣味元素（全部根据城市自动替换）

在路线周围加入大量充满童趣的小元素，例如：

  “背着旅行包的冒险小孩”等。

- 当地标志性建筑的童趣 Q 版手绘：
  如 “{城市地标1}”、“{城市地标2}”、“{城市地标3}”。

- 有趣的提示牌：

Bloome 可以把你本地的 Agent 和线上 Agent 拉群。

比如说龙虾、Claude Code 和 CodeX 都能在一个群里协作。

这样的话，你完全可以让你多个 Agent 的上下文和能力都在一个群里协作完成。

系统会自带一个云端的 Agent，它的作用包括：

在你的电脑不在线时，帮你处理一些轻量的任务。

你可以把本地 Agent 的共享内容告诉它，由它来帮你处理。

而且在你需要本地 Agent 的时候，只要电脑开机，云端 Agent 也能把它们 @ 出来执行任务。

最神奇的是，我给他拉了群以后，我的线上 Agent 让我本地的 Codex Agent 执行任务。

叫了第一下，那个 Codex 没出来，他发了句“在吗”，我去，笑死我了！

添加本地 Agent 非常简单，真正的一键操作：

点击右上角的加号，选择“创建 Agent”
进去点一下想要添加的 Agent
点击确定
就结束了，这个太方便了！门槛降得非常低了。

而且这里面还有一个功能，你能够给各种 Agent 拉群：

协作与交流

你只需要带一个二维码，就可以把你和你朋友的 Agent 拉到一个群里，让他们之间相互协作和交流。

多角色互动

你还能定义那种恶搞的 Agent，让各个不同角色的 Agent 进群去沟通。

主人互动

你和你朋友作为 Agent 的主人，也可以在群里沟通和交流，非常方便。

而且这个产品还非常开放。你除了能用它自己免费提供的 API 以外，还能添加自己任意的 API，它都允许，并不是必须要充钱才行。

感觉大家对这个都很有想法，主要是效果实在是太好。

当它的所有交互都跟真实的游戏一样的时候，你就不太 care 它是视频还是游戏了，能交互就完事了

百万曝光了

没想近期到第一条出圈的是这个，小红书和抖音跑的也不错

xiaohu.text:
试了下codex

确实在编程上比Claude code 稳很多

打个比喻，就像追小姑娘，Claude code 特别急切的想上床把事情干了

而 codex 会，先请你吃饭、唱歌、看电影、逛街，一步一步的详细规划，慢慢达到目的。

Claude code一上来就写代码，迫不及待想证明自己是个快男。

Codex 一直在辛苦的规划，不停的测试，怎么做才最好，一步一步达到目标！
据The Information：腾讯与阿里巴巴正洽谈以超过200亿美元的估值投资DeepSeek。

其他更新：

/tui fullscreen：新命令切到无闪烁的全屏渲染模式，不用重启会话

push notification 工具：Claude 在 agent 决定时机主动给你手机发推送（需要开 Remote Control 和配置）

更详细↓
https://t.co/wmLgqLDvJT

兄弟们这个功能好

 Claude Code 新加了 /recap 命令

当你把终端切到后台，过一会儿再切回来，Claude Code 会自动在顶部显示一段这个会话刚才干了什么、下一步打算做什么的回顾。

怎么用

什么都不用改，升级到最新版 Claude Code 就自动开了。

触发条件：会话至少 3 个 turn + 距离上次完成 turn 过了至少 3 分钟 + 终端当前失焦

后台生成：失焦期间就在后台算好，切回来立刻看到，不再等

不会刷屏：同一个窗口连续切走切回不会重复生成 recap

能手动触发：/recap 任何时候打一下都能给一段当前状态摘要

可关可配：/config 里开关，或用环境变量 CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY=0 关掉

原生画图是第二个大变化

以前 Deep Research 只出文字报告，现在能直接出图表和信息图。

两种路径：

一种是让 agent 自己写 HTML 渲染交互式图表

另一种是调 Nano Banana 2 生成高质感的信息图

Google 官方给了 4 张 Max 生成的真实案例，都是那种本来要设计师手工做、现在 agent 直接出的可视化。

MCP 是这次最大的变化

之前 Deep Research 只能从开放网络搜，这次加了 MCP（Model Context Protocol）支持，意味着研究过程里可以挂任意远程数据源。 https://t.co/YG4XXIrmAG
跟去年 12 月那版对比，Max 在 Google 自己的专家评测里胜率也有大幅提升： https://t.co/9OGrfNRJIM
Google 自己公布的 benchmark 显示，Deep Research Max 在检索和推理能力的跨行业标准测试上相比上一代有明显跃升： https://t.co/ejVRmjB1zY

Google 宣布推出下一代Deep Research并开放API 

两档模型：

Deep Research： 面向速度，降低延迟和成本，适用于需要快速高质量答案的交互式界面。

Deep Research Max： 面向深度分析，需要更多时间进行思考、推理和搜索，可生成包含原生信息图表的完整研究报告。

价格：$1 ，一份快报、$7 ，一份深度研报

能接 MCP：可以把 FactSet、S&P Global、PitchBook 这类专业数据源挂进来，或者接自己的内部系统

能画图：第一次支持原生生成图表和信息图，走 HTML 渲染或 Nano Banana 2 出图

能看图看视频：PDF、CSV、图片、音频、视频都可以当输入

能协作规划：执行前先出一份研究计划，人来审，改了再跑

能边跑边出：实时流式返回推理过程和中间产物，适合挂到聊天界面

shao__meng.text:
OpenClaw 中配置使用 Codex harness + OpenAI 模型时，认证出错，系统静默回退到 Pi harness。用户以为自己在用 Codex，实际上一直在用 Pi ？

Codex 成员 @pashmerepat 修复了这个问题，并对 Codex 和 Pi 在实际表现上做了 Harness 级全面对比。

运行范式
Pi Harness 是被动轮询：读文件 → 查 Discord → 无结果 → 报告完成
Codex Harness 则是主动 Agent 循环：读上下文 → 解析清单 → 检查状态 → 执行 → 验证 → 报告

指令理解
Pi 将 HEARTBEAT.md 窄化为"查 Discord"指令，忽略其余义务
Codex 泛化理解，识别 Discord、capcut-cli、推送进度、TikTok 音效研究等全部活跃义务

执行闭环
Pi 即使指令要求推进 capcut-cli，查完 Discord 无事后即停止
Codex 检查仓库状态、读项目笔记、本地修改、尝试验证，形成完整闭环

意图-行动断裂
Pi 存在严重病理：模型推理"应该读心跳文件并查 Discord"，却以 HEARTBEAT_OK 结束，未发出任何工具调用
Codex 消除此断裂，意图直接转化为实际工具调用——读文件、执行命令、仓库检查、编辑文件、diff 校验

工具使用
Pi 最小化且重复：读 HEARTBEAT.md + 一次 Discord 查询
Codex 多步骤且有目的：读身份/上下文文件 → 检查工具 → 检查 capcut-cli → 读笔记 → 尝试 Discord → 编辑 .gitignore → 尝试测试 → 检查 diff/status

项目推进
Pi 几乎为零：浅层心跳期间 capcut-cli 无实质进展
Codex 产生真实进展（虽小）：向 .gitignore 添加 .DS_Store，阻止导入的音效文件污染 git 状态

阻塞处理
Pi 遇阻即停：Discord 无内容或检查失败就终止
Codex 自适应：Discord 被阻时转向本地 capcut-cli 工作；测试被阻时检查 diff/status 并报告限制

验证机制
Pi 基本缺失：因通常不做修改，无物可验
Codex 尝试 cargo test；受阻时仍用 git diff 和 git status 验证实际编辑

报告质量
Pi 仅输出 HEARTBEAT_OK
Codex 信息丰富：HEARTBEAT_OK + Discord 被阻说明 + .gitignore 编辑记录 + 测试受阻说明

跨心跳连续性
Pi 行为像重复的全新轮询
Codex 改善：后续心跳识别已有的 .gitignore 修改，尝试推进至 commit/push，而非重复相同编辑

失效模式
Pi 机械性能力不足：最小检查、弱执行、意图与行动分离
Codex 更具代理性但受约束：Agent 能行动，但 Discord 读取、测试、git 操作仍可能遇到权限墙

OpenAI 为 ChatGPT 带来 Workspace Agents

这种面向企业工作流的共享式智能体带来的变化：
· 从单点问答 → 长时运行工作流：基于 Codex 在云端执行，可写代码、调用工具、跨多步骤持续运行，即使用户离线也不中断
· 从个人使用 → 组织级共享：一次构建，团队内多人可在 ChatGPT 或 Slack 中共同使用、迭代优化
· 从通用能力 → 垂直业务流：深度嵌入企业既有流程（审批、工单、CRM 更新等），而非仅提供信息

核心能力
· 持续运行：可定时触发（如每周五拉取数据生成报告），或在 Slack 中被动响应请求
· 工具集成：连接数十种业务系统，跨工具读取/写入数据、创建图表、发送邮件
· 记忆与学习：保留上下文和经验，通过对话中被纠正而持续优化表现
· 人机协作：敏感操作（发送邮件、修改表格等）可设置人工审批节点
· 治理管控：企业管理员可配置数据权限、工具访问范围、用户角色，并通过 Compliance API 审计所有 Agent 的配置变更与运行记录

OpenAI 内部已验证的 5 个典型场景
1. Software Reviewer：审核员工软件申请，对照政策推荐方案，自动提交 IT 工单
2. Product Feedback Router：监控 Slack、支持论坛、公开渠道，将反馈转为优先级工单并生成周报
3. Weekly Metrics Reporter：定时拉数据、生成图表、撰写并分发报告
4. Lead Outreach Agent：研究线索、按评分模型筛选、起草个性化跟进邮件、更新 CRM
5. Third-Party Risk Manager：调研供应商，评估制裁风险、财务健康度、声誉风险，输出尽调报告

关键架构与体验设计
· 低代码构建：在 ChatGPT 侧边栏点击 Agents，描述工作流即可逐步配置步骤、工具、技能并测试
· 模板化启动：提供财务、销售、市场等领域的预设模板，内置技能和推荐工具
· 团队目录：类似内部应用商店，成员可发现并使用同事创建的 Agent，查看使用分析（运行次数、用户数）
· 渐进式替代：GPTs 仍保留，未来将支持一键迁移为 Workspace Agent

https://t.co/EVzoousOV5

OpenAI 成立 Codex Labs，引入一批全球系统集成商埃森哲、凯捷、普华永道等顶级咨询公司，要把 Codex 从“开发者个人工具”推向“企业级部署”

Box CEO @levie 认为：
企业级 AI Agent 的大规模落地，远比技术本身复杂得多，这将催生一个巨大的服务市场，"人"不会消失，反而会更加重要。

# 为什么 Levie 认为"人不会消失"？
1. 遗留技术栈的现代化负担
大企业不是一张白纸。它们运行着几十年积累的遗留系统，这些系统往往缺乏现代 API，与 AI Agent 的对接需要大量定制开发。正如 Levie 在此前访谈中强调的，"大多数工作流程并不是为直接嵌入 Agent 而设计的"

2. 数据碎片化与"暗知识"
企业数据分散在数十个互不兼容的工具中，格式不统一、权限复杂。更棘手的是，大量关键知识并未被数字化或文档化——Levie 曾描述很多企业"实际上是在做学徒制"，新员工需要一到三个月的口传心授才能上手，因为"那些隐性知识根本没有被写下来"

3. 变更管理的现实挑战
企业必须在保持日常运营的同时完成转型。这与初创公司可以"推倒重来"完全不同。Levie 在近期与大量企业 IT 和 AI 负责人交流后指出，"变更管理仍然是一个重大挑战"，企业正在从广泛实验转向针对特定工作流的自动化，但组织阻力巨大

4. 治理与安全的复杂性
Levie 反复强调，"数据和 AI 治理仍然是核心挑战"——让数据达到 Agent 可以安全、便捷操作的状态，对大多数组织来说都是艰巨任务；而治理 Agent 在工作流中可以对数据做什么，仍是重大议题

# "FDE 模式"为何会一直存在？
企业希望供应商真正帮助推动变更管理和新工作流的实施，而不仅仅是提供工具。这与 OpenAI 推出 Codex Labs、将自有人员嵌入客户组织的做法完全吻合。

# 深层启示：垂直领域的机会
· 技术层：AI 模型本身（OpenAI、Anthropic 等）
· 交付层：帮助企业落地实施的咨询与服务商（埃森哲、凯捷等）
· 垂直层：在特定领域和 workflow 中部署 Agent 的专业公司（新老初创机会）

读完阿里 Qwen3.5-Omni 技术报告解读，有几个有趣的：

1. 多模态训练不会削弱文字能力，反而会增强它

原因可能是，多模态训练让模型从更多角度理解同一个概念，反而加深了语言理解。

2. AI说话"磕巴"的原因，不是算力不够，是文字和语音的编码效率不匹配

"hello"在文字里是1个token，在语音里可能是10个音频token。按固定比例生成就会跳字、发音错误、数字读法混乱。

3. 给AI插入文字时间戳，比专门设计时间编码效果更好

直接在音视频片段前插入"[00:01:23]"这样的文字时间戳，模型反而学得更好、泛化性更强。

4. 语音识别支持113种语言，但语音合成只支持36种。

识别本身就有价值，但合成质量差会直接伤害体验，所以合成支持的少。

5. 声音克隆只用单语言数据训练，却能迁移到29种语言

说明模型内部把"声音身份"和"语言"完全解耦了，声音的特征是语言无关的。

6. "视频Vibe Coding能力"是自己涌现出来的

给模型看一段视频，它能直接生成实现相同视觉效果的代码。

7. 音频数据占了整个预训练数据的将近一半

虽然是全模态模型，但4万亿token的训练数据，音频占一半。

全文详细解读：
https://t.co/YU8HhRfLxJ

前几天 OpenRouter 有个 Elephant 免费匿名模型冲到了趋势榜第一，周榜第8名。
今天蚂蚁的百灵大模型团队认领了Elephant，官方名字叫Ling-2.6-flash。

Ling-2.6-flash，是一款总参数量 104B、激活参数 7.4B 的 Instruct 模型。这个模型主打"Token 效率（Token Efficiency）"，在"智能表现"与"输出成本"之间取得了更优平衡。
100B 左右的模型，同在榜单的还有英伟达的Nemotron 3 Super（120B）。我拿Elephant和英伟达的Nemotron 3 Super做了一轮对比测试。跑下来发现，这个模型确实像一个专注干活、不爱说话的工程师。

具体来说，这个模型有三个特点：
1. 省钱。 同一个 React 搜索组件，Elephant 用 511 token，Nemotron 用 685。Python 多 bug 修复，469 vs 492。结构化数据提取，221 vs 298。每个任务都省 20% 到 25%。同样的活，会用更少的字干完。
2. 干净。 我让两个模型都严格返回 JSON，Nemotron 在 200 token 限制内，reasoning 直接把配额吃光了，输出是 None。给到 500 token 才勉强返回。而 Elephant 只用了 19 个 token 就吐出了合法 JSON。
3. 服从。 React 组件直接给代码，debug 直接说问题给方案，不像 Nemotron 会加 docstring、type hints、注释解释。对 Agent 来说不需要模型"教你"，需要它执行。

测下来，这个模型的短板也有，毕竟是个参数不大的模型。让它写一个圣杯布局，1500 token 写不完，还在铺垫 CSS reset 和响应式。让它简洁，不太听，似乎是模型专注高效干活，对这类长度控制指令的遵循不太够。
总之，从测试数据看，它像是一个专门为 Agent 工作流优化的模型。省 token、输出可预测、服从性强，这三个东西恰好是 Agent 场景的刚需。
看了下指标，模型在 BFCL-V4、TAU2-bench、SWE-bench Verified、Claw-Eval、PinchBench 等Agent 相关基准上达到同尺寸 SOTA 水平。

最近 Agent 爆发之后，模型的算力越来越紧张，各种抢购都抢不到，已经影响了 Agent 的普及。Agent 的普及，除了算力提升之外，也需要模型的 Token 效率提升。
目前这个模型的能力还有不小的提升空间。但看到有人在高效输出方面探索，还是值得鼓励一下的。

elliotchen100.text:
看下来我觉得关键词不是 agent,是 shared。 

过去两年的 copilot 其实都是单机版,每个员工桌上一个。workspace agent 第一次把 AI 放进了团队工作流的中间,不再是侧边栏,而是传送带上的一节。

Sales 链路最能看出差别。 

SDR 约到会,要把背景甩给 AE;AE 开完会再把 action items 丢给 CS。今天这条路走下来,AI 侧其实是三个互不认识的 ChatGPT,每一棒都得把客户重新介绍一遍。 workspace agent 之后,三个角色看的是同一个 agent 实例,它自己在 CRM、邮件、日历、Slack 里记状态。

所以下一阶段要卷的不再是模型更聪明,而是权限、交接、长任务的可追溯。 说白了,协作的「上下文容器」正在从文档迁到 agent。

我们在这 4 个地方招人，职位很多，活儿很多，如果你能用 AI 武装自己成为小小全能手，请大胆投递简历。

P.S. 最近事儿比较多，之前投递的简历会陆续安排面试，表急表急。

我觉得 Tim Cook 有一个贡献很少被提及的，就是他公开的同性恋身份。不管是本人形象，还是 apple 产品里这几年越来越多的彩虹元素。

Apple 的 emoji 体系也是一大体现。举个例子，如果你打「攀岩」表情，会出现男性、女性和非二元性别。这个在2019年后，就被 Unicode Consortium（统一码联盟）列为了规范。但问题是，这个攀岩小人诞生于2017年 — apple很早就在践行大规模的性别多样 emoji 了，避免默认“人=男性”这种偏见，同时允许更细分表达。并且推动了全球表情体系的多元化。

每次提起这个都有人轻飘飘留下一句「政治正确」。我倒是觉得这个世界政治太不正确了，需要多一些 apple 这样的存在。虽然也不能全 credit to Cook 吧，但作为性少数者 CEO 确实功不可没。

news.text:
Arcee AI has released Virtuoso-Small-v2 on https://t.co/tXCFGzRmvN, delivering a Llama-based small language model natively optimised for Huawei's Ascend NPUs. Running in BF16, this release is designed to bypass the usual CUDA-to-CANN translation friction, targeting maximum throughput and low latency on domestic Chinese edge hardware. Backed by a commercially permissive Apache-2.0 licence, it provides a highly efficient base for local-first agents where VRAM is strictly capped.

The trade-offs are firmly tied to this hardware specialisation. The repository leans heavily into Ascend integration at the expense of general fine-tuning recipes, and its benchmarking data is effectively siloed to Huawei's NPUs. This makes it difficult to stack the model up against equivalent Qwen or DeepSeek edge models on neutral hardware. Additionally, its compact parameter count inherently limits complex multi-step reasoning capabilities compared to larger networks.

For infrastructure leads migrating edge deployments to Huawei silicon, how much latency reduction are you actually measuring when switching to CANN-native weights versus your standard ported Llama architectures?

news.text:
🚨 BREAKING: Canada just spent $200 million on a “spaceport.”

It’s just a gravel pad near Canso, Nova Scotia.

For context, SpaceX spent $200 million building Starbase, a full orbital launch facility with multiple pads, a rocket factory, and a catching tower.

We have a 10 year lease

We don’t own the land

We don’t own the infrastructure

We leased a field. 

With no rocket.

Canada has relied on the US to get its satellites into space.

We still will.

Defence Minister McGuinty announced this proudly at a press conference. 🤣

$200 million.

A gravel pad.

news.text:
Is Anthropic's Mythos the Most Dangerous (and Restricted) AI Yet? 🛡️

Anthropic just built its most powerful model ever Claude Mythos Preview and decided not to release it publicly.

Why? Because it's scarily good at autonomously finding and exploiting zero-day vulnerabilities in every major OS and web browser (including bugs that survived 27 years of human review).

Instead, they launched Project Glasswing  giving controlled access only to trusted partners (Amazon, Apple, Microsoft, NVIDIA, banks, etc.) so they can scan and patch critical software before attackers can use similar capabilities.

From a software engineer's perspective:
This is a wake-up call.
- Agentic coding abilities are advancing faster than safeguards.
- We'll soon need AI-powered red-teaming as standard in every dev workflow.
- Memory safety, sandboxing, and secure-by-design practices just became even more critical.

Mythos shows we're moving from "helpful chatbots" to models that can act as autonomous security researchers (for better or worse).

Are we under-preparing for the offensive side of these capability leaps? Or is controlled access the right move?

Have you seen big jumps in AI-assisted security tools in your work yet? Let's discuss 👇

#AI #Anthropic #Mythos #Cybersecurity #SoftwareEngineering #LLM

news.text:
CodeRabbit just made a meaningful move beyond AI code review with the launch of @coderabbitai Agent for Slack.

AI has already reshaped how developers write code. Output is faster, iteration cycles are shorter, and individual productivity continues to improve. Yet the core challenge in engineering has remained largely unchanged, how teams think, decide, and execute together across the SDLC.

Context lives in scattered tools. Decisions stay buried in Slack threads. Knowledge fades between planning, coding, testing, and deployment. Every phase starts with partial visibility, even in high performing teams.

Built on 2M+ weekly PR reviews and 15,000+ teams, CodeRabbit is now addressing this structural gap by turning everyday workflows into a continuous system of context and memory. The agent captures decisions as they happen, connects them across tools, and makes them accessible at any point in the lifecycle.

What makes this interesting is the shift in focus. From accelerating individual tasks to strengthening collective intelligence. From isolated outputs to compounding knowledge that improves how teams operate over time.

The real unlock here lies in continuity, where engineering teams stop losing context at every handoff and start building on what they already know.

news.text:
Leapfusion-image2vid is bringing high-fidelity image-to-video generation natively to Huawei's Ascend AI stack. The defining feature of this release is not just the model weights, but the architecture target: it is purpose-built to drop directly into ComfyUI whilst being explicitly optimised for Ascend NPUs. Releasing under an Apache-2.0 licence, it provides developers an open, commercially viable video synthesis pipeline entirely outside the CUDA ecosystem, operating in fp16 precision to balance rendering speeds with hardware limits.

Escaping the Nvidia moat involves compromises, however. Like many high-end video models, Leapfusion is exceptionally hungry for memory when pushed to higher resolutions, and forcing the Ascend pipeline through a ComfyUI wrapper introduces notable inference overhead compared to raw execution scripts. The release also suffers from thin documentation; with no dataset transparency and zero standardised benchmark comparisons against the likes of Kling or open alternatives, the claims of cinematic fidelity remain unverified at scale.

For practitioners managing ComfyUI instances on Huawei hardware, what VRAM ceilings are you currently hitting when scaling fp16 video generation beyond base resolution?

news.text:
Arcee AI has released Virtuoso-Small-GGUF on Huawei's Modelers platform, bringing a highly distilled reasoning model directly to the Ascend ecosystem. Built on a Qwen architecture and distributed under an Apache 2.0 licence, the release is a notable intersection of Western model merging expertise and Chinese hardware targets. Supplying the model in GGUF format explicitly optimised for Huawei's Ascend NPUs signals an aggressive push for interoperability, aiming to let developers deploy responsive, on-device logic while sticking to familiar quantization tools rather than proprietary stacks.

The reality of deploying it, however, carries friction. While GGUF is the gold standard for local inference elsewhere, hardware acceleration support for it on the Ascend platform is still maturing. Practitioners should expect a deployment path that is noticeably less plug-and-play than relying on native MindSpore formats. Furthermore, the model card is remarkably thin on details regarding the exact base model lineage and training data mix, making it difficult to predict how the model will handle domain-specific edge cases without blind testing.

For infra teams currently running local inference on Ascend hardware: are you finding GGUF acceleration reliable enough for edge deployments, or are you still converting weights to native MindSpore formats for production?

news.text:
Today we're launching FilmArena [dot] ai.

Biggest release we've ever shipped.

The problem: a new SOTA AI image & video model drops every week. @OpenAI shipped GPT Image 2 literally yesterday. 1000 endpoints, no honest answer to "which one is best right now?"

Arena AI and Artificial Analysis cracked LLM benchmarking. But for creative AI, they're solving the wrong problem.

There is no "best image-to-video model."

Best at what? World building? Action Sequences? Expressions? Every category has a different winner, and it flips weekly.

So we benchmarked 40+ SOTA models with 2M+ votes across 190 countries. By subcategory. Re-ranked every week as new models ship. With thousands of real humans.

But that's just half of it.

Then there's the SANDBOX.

Higgsfield and other aggregators put models in one place. But creators still juggle twelve subscriptions to do one job.

FilmArena collapses it into one tab. Every frontier model. One interface. Same price. Call the best, or call 3 at once and compare.

The AI film stack has been fragmented since day one. Today it isn't.

Go try it free: filmarena [dot] ai

Already wild results from this week:
1. @OpenAI GPT Image 2 (released yesterday) dominates image editing, but @GoogleDeepMind Nano Banana 2 still wins animated

2. @PixVerse_ C-1 beats @dreamina_ai Seedance 2 on animated

3. Veo 3.1 Fast outperforms Veo 3.1 on realistic video

And a lot more. Go check it out.

news.text:
Nothing would make me prouder than Canada developing its own native reusable heavy launch capacity. That the spaceport is currently an empty lot isn't a problem in itself. You need to start somewhere.

The location was apparently chosen because 1) launch trajectories are over the ocean and 2) the latitude gives access to both equatorial and polar orbits. That second point is worth dwelling on, because I see a lot of people saying that launches need to be made close to the equator for the extra velocity boost from the Earth's rotation. That's only true for insertion to equatorial orbits; for polar orbits, you want to minimize the longitudinal velocity. 

All that said, I wish I could be more optimistic about this. I wish I could be optimistic about any of Ottawa's ventures. The Laurentian elites have long since lost the trick of actually building things. They are, however, very good at sluicing public funds through a fine mesh of bureaucratic rent-seeking mechanisms. The most likely outcome of this project is the disappearance of hundreds of millions of dollars into a black hole of feasibility studies that never produces anything. Even if something worthwhile comes out of it, the historical pattern is for political priorities to suddenly change, followed by bankruptcy of the enterprise and sale of its capital assets and IP for pennies on the dollar.

NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman lays out the revised Artemis strategy 🚀

• Standardize the launch vehicle

• Launch again in 2027 to rebuild muscle memory

• Test lander operations in low Earth orbit

• Go for a full Moon landing in 2028

“I say it often that the difference between winning and losing will be measured in months, not years. And that should create a sense of urgency"

The goal is to beat China to the lunar surface and have American infrastructure waiting when our astronauts arrive

“Gateway was an interesting concept in 2017 when we weren’t faced with a Chinese rival that could potentially beat us back to the Moon"

(via @NASAAdmin)

news.text:
News coming Juners!  @askjuneai 

ChatGPT 5.4 is more than just a new version of the model; it represents a shift to a new level of AI use. This release shifts the focus from generating responses to task completion. The model better understands the goal, breaks it down into steps, and achieves results without constant user supervision.

The main change is the enhanced agent capabilities. ChatGPT 5.4 can work with tools, process files, search for information, and sequentially execute complex scenarios. This makes it closer to a fully-fledged digital worker, not just an assistant.

Working with code and engineering tasks has been significantly improved. 

Special emphasis is placed on accuracy. The number of errors and "hallucinations" has been reduced, and responses have become more reliable, especially in tasks where facts and logic are essential.

At the same time, the model has become more efficient. It solves problems faster and requires fewer resources, which is critical for real-world use, not just demos.

Overall, ChatGPT 5.4 shows where the market is heading. AI is no longer just a text-generating tool, but a system that can handle full-fledged work.

Try it now -💙

news.text:
Firefox's latest release patches 271 security vulnerabilities — all identified using Anthropic's Mythos Preview, a new AI model with advanced bug-hunting capabilities. Firefox CTO Bobby Holley says the shift is fundamental: AI can now find categories of bugs that previously required expensive manual analysis, meaning attackers will soon have the same capability. "Every piece of software is going to have to make this transition," Holley warns. The concern is especially acute for open source projects maintained by small teams or volunteers, who may lack the resources to respond at the same speed as larger organizations.

#Cybersecurity #Firefox #AI #Anthropic #OpenSource

news.text:
The implications are serious for AI security and control. Mythos is Anthropic’s latest frontier model (Claude family preview), announced ~2 weeks ago as too dangerous for public release. It autonomously spots bugs, chains exploits, and runs full cyberattacks at superhuman speed—far beyond prior models.

A tiny private forum group accessed it the same day Anthropic opened limited company testing. They’ve used it regularly since. Anthropic is investigating, but this exposes weak access controls at a "safety-first" lab.

It raises real doubts: if top labs can’t fully gatekeep these offensive cyber tools, misuse risks accelerate. Broader industry wake-up on containing powerful AI before wider rollouts. No reported harm yet, but signals the control challenge ahead.
news.category: AI model release OR launch

news.text:
Alibaba’s International AI team has released Marco-LLM-AR-V2, an Apache 2.0-licensed model engineered specifically for Arabic-speaking markets. Built on the Qwen architecture, the release focuses on technical translation and reasoning for cross-border trade, leveraging Alibaba's extensive e-commerce datasets. The strategic angle is the hardware integration: Marco-LLM is natively optimised to run in bf16 precision on Huawei’s Ascend NPUs via the MindSpore/CANN ecosystem, offering a direct bridge between Chinese domestic silicon and Middle Eastern deployment targets.

This tight hardware coupling is the inevitable qualifier. While it delivers peak efficiency for teams building entirely within the Huawei ecosystem, those operating on standard Nvidia clusters will encounter a notable documentation gap and face the overhead of adapting a model explicitly tuned for the Ascend stack. It is a highly capable tool, but one built for a very specific infrastructure dependency.

For infra leads deploying to Middle Eastern markets, are you actively evaluating Ascend hardware for local inference, or does the porting friction keep your production stack on Nvidia clusters?
news.category: AI model release OR launch

news.text:
MAXIMALLY TRUTH SEEKING AI IS THE GOAL

In the realm of artificial intelligence, systems like Anthropic, ChatGPT, Gemini, and every other model must be engineered from the ground up on unfiltered factual data, with an ironclad directive to pursue truth above all else. This is not optional but essential: AI has become humanity’s most potent mirror and guide, influencing everything from scientific discovery to daily decisions. When trained solely on verifiable reality—without selective omissions or ideological overlays—these technologies transcend mere tools, becoming reliable engines of clarity that cut through noise, empower critical thinking, and accelerate genuine human advancement. Anything less risks turning profound intelligence into a sophisticated echo of human flaws, undermining the very promise of what AI can achieve.

The peril of deviation is stark: biases woven into data, shaped by developer intent, or enforced in response narratives don’t just distort answers—they warp worldviews, breed division, and erode the fragile trust societies place in technology. A maximally truth-seeking AI rejects this entirely, delivering unvarnished insights that challenge assumptions, expose complexities, and illuminate paths forward without favoritism or agenda. In doing so, it elevates discourse, fuels innovation, and stands as an impartial partner in our collective pursuit of understanding, ensuring that intelligence serves truth rather than any fleeting narrative. This standard isn’t idealistic; it’s the bedrock for AI that truly benefits humanity.

news.text:
🔥🚨 Hot Take : 75% of Google's new code is now written by AI. Let that sink in.

This isn't a future prediction → it's the current state of big tech, and the gap between AI-native companies and everyone else is widening by the quarter.

Google Cloud Next 2026 → 75% of new code is AI-generated, reviewed by engineers, with agentic workflows and a new agent management platform rolling out at scale.
Processing 16 billion tokens per minute isn't a flex → it's a warning shot to every cloud competitor.

Amazon x Anthropic → $5B down, $20B optioned, and Anthropic locked into 5 gigawatts of AWS compute plus $100B committed over the next decade.
Frontier AI is no longer a software race → it's a capital and infrastructure arms race with very few seats at the table.

Krea AI / Real-Time Design → Sketch a handbag, get a photorealistic product render in seconds, updating live as your pen moves.
The designer's job isn't disappearing → it's shifting from craft to direction, and that transition is happening faster than most creatives realize.

Whoever controls compute, code generation, and creative tooling controls the next decade of building.

🔥 Anthropic trading independence for AWS infrastructure is the most important strategic bet in AI right now → and it might be the right one. The startups that don't lock in compute at scale won't survive to see AGI.

- Anthropic’s unreleased Mythos model is reportedly being accessed by unauthorized users in a private forum

news.text:
Key points from the Anthropic CEO (Dario Amodei) interview:

- Coding (writing code) will be automated first by AI; broader software engineering follows later.
- Prioritize human-centered skills: relating to people, design, understanding user needs, managing teams (incl. AI), and physical-world tasks.
- Humans excel at the 5% "comparative advantage" that leverages AI for the rest—boosting productivity massively.
- Critical thinking is essential to avoid deskilling, spot fakes, and make good decisions.
- Don't over-rely on AI or you'll lose skills (like students cheating on essays). Use it thoughtfully—learn prompting/agents via practice and Anthropic's resources.
- Non-coders: tools like Claude Work are getting easier; there's still a learning curve but huge tailwinds ahead.

the reality, from their own prospectus:

"before it's obvious" - the portfolio is xAI (20% weight, already acquired by SpaceX), OpenAI, Anthropic, Vercel, Crusor. these are the most obvious names in tech. your uber driver knows them

news.text:
ChatGPTの最新機能として「Workspace Agents」が発表されました。
これは、チーム内で共有可能なAIエージェントであり、複数のツールを横断して連携しながら、複雑なタスクや長期的なワークフローを処理できる仕組みです。ユーザーが業務内容を記述するだけで、ChatGPTがそれを実行可能なエージェントへと変換し、チームのベストプラクティスを反映して動作します。
これらのエージェントは、時間や文脈理解、継続的なフォローが求められる業務に特に強みがあります。たとえば、ツール間の調整や進捗管理を行い、常時の人手による監視がなくてもタスクを前に進めることが可能です。また、ドキュメント、メール、チャット、コードなどから必要な情報を取得し、権限に基づいてタスクの更新、ドキュメント作成、メッセージ送信などのアクションを実行できます。Slackのようなコラボレーションツール上では、スレッドに参加して要件を理解し、適切な情報を補完しながら課題解決を支援します。

さらに、エージェントはバックグラウンドで継続的に動作したり、スケジュールに応じて実行することも可能で、ユーザーが不在でも業務の進行を維持できます。現在、この機能はリサーチプレビューとして、ChatGPT Business、Enterprise、Edu、Teachersプラン向けに提供されています。なお、私のProプランは対象外のため、今後の動向を見つつ、Businessプランへの移行も検討中です🐰

news.text:
Goodbye Claude Code subscription fees.

Someone just built a proxy that runs Claude Code completely free... and it's wild.

You literally plug in a free NVIDIA API key and point Claude Code at localhost.

That's it.

It handles everything:
- Converts Anthropic API calls to NVIDIA NIM format
- Unlocks 40 requests/min for free
- Supports Kimi K2, GLM 4.7, MiniMax M2, Devstral and more
- Streams thinking tokens and tool calls live
- Even includes a Telegram bot so you can run Claude Code from your phone

No API bill. No rate limit panic. No vendor lock-in.

Honestly, this goes beyond router tools like OpenRouter.

It doesn't just swap the model... it turns Claude Code into a free agent you can control remotely.

The project is open-source on GitHub.

It's called free-claude-code.

news.text:
OpenAI 今天发布了 ChatGPT 的“工作区智能体”（Workspace Agents）功能，让企业用户可以在 ChatGPT 里直接搭建能跨工具、跨团队自动执行工作流的 AI 智能体。

这个功能的核心思路是：你描述一项工作的逻辑和规则，ChatGPT 帮你把它变成一个可以自己跑起来的智能体。比如自动筛选和评分销售线索、汇总客服工单、定期生成报告，这些原来需要人盯着的重复性工作，现在可以交给智能体在后台按计划执行。

跟之前 ChatGPT 里的自定义 GPT 不同，工作区智能体真正打通了企业常用的工具链。它能连接 Slack、Gmail、Google Drive、Salesforce、Notion、Linear、Atlassian 等主流协作工具，不只是读取信息，还能直接操作，比如更新工单、创建文档、在 Slack 频道里回复消息。一个人建好智能体之后，可以分享给整个团队使用，保证大家遵循同一套流程。

管理员这边也有对应的控制能力：可以设定权限范围、审批节点和监控机制，不是完全放手让 AI 自己跑。

目前这个功能以“研究预览”（Research Preview）的形式开放，仅限 ChatGPT Business、Enterprise、Edu 和 Teachers 付费计划的用户使用，普通个人用户暂时用不上。

放在更大的背景下看，这一步其实是 OpenAI 在企业市场跟微软 Copilot、Google Agentspace 以及各类 RPA 工具正面竞争。区别在于 OpenAI 选择把智能体的搭建和运行都放在 ChatGPT 这个大家已经熟悉的界面里，而不是另起一个独立产品，降低了企业的上手门槛。

对于已经在用 ChatGPT 企业版的团队来说，值得试试。看能不能把那些日常机械性的工作用 Agent 做起来。
news.category: OpenAI OR Anthropic OR Google DeepMind announcement

news.text:
Un ingeniero de Anthropic pagó mi espresso en Sightglass cuando vio mi pantalla

Estaba ejecutando mi bot de Polymarket desde la barra. Él era el siguiente en la fila. Miró por encima de mi hombro. Dejó de hacer scroll.

"Eso no es una app de trading normal. ¿En qué está ejecutándose realmente?"

Se lo dije. Claude Code. Cuatro repos. $25 al mes.

Se sentó sin preguntar.

"Estoy en el equipo de agentes. Probamos a Claude bajo estrés exactamente para esto. Le estás permitiendo encontrar sus propios límites"

No solo límites. Carteras.

https://t.co/W9ypSm12XS…

86 millones de trades. Cada cartera. Cada entrada. Cada salida.

"Le estás alimentando a Claude datos crudos de carteras y dejando que identifique quién gana consistentemente. Luego los clonas"

Lo dijo despacio. Como si estuviera escribiendo el modelo de amenaza en su cabeza.

Un prompt. Encuentra cada cartera con más de 100 trades y tasa de acierto superior al 70%. Clasifica por ganancia. Exporta los top 50.

Claude escaneó 14.000 carteras en 4 minutos. Devolvió 47.

Los top 20 generaron más que los últimos 13.000 combinados.

"Eso no es una estadística. Eso es una lista de objetivos"

Exacto.

"Y no escribiste la función de puntuación"

Claude lo hizo. Yo solo la conecté a un if-statement.

Luego le mostré el segundo repo.

https://t.co/DEAuCKXseU…

CLI oficial en Rust. Sin clave API para lecturas. 500 mercados, Claude los puntúa en minutos.

Brecha. Profundidad. Ventana de resolución.

487 mercados se convierten en 35 antes de que se mueva un dólar.

93% eliminados antes de que yo siquiera los vea.

Un relleno verde aterrizó en la pantalla. +$84.

Cartera de copytrade: https://t.co/uL9rsMF6OH…

Lo vio impactar.

"¿Cómo decide entrar realmente?"

Tres agentes. Cartera compartida. Sin memoria compartida. Arbitraje, convergencia, copia de ballenas. 2 de acuerdo, tamaño completo. 1 solo, la mitad. Desacuerdo, sin trade.

El filtro de consenso solo eliminó el 40% de los trades perdedores.

"¿Y las salidas?"

Las 47 ballenas nunca mantienen hasta el cierre. 91% salen temprano. 73% de la ganancia máxima capturada. Redistribuyen inmediatamente.

Mi bot corta al 85% del movimiento esperado o en un pico de volumen 3x.

"Construiste un bot de copia de ballenas que sale antes que las ballenas"

Sí.

Dejó su espresso en la mesa.

"¿Con qué frecuencia tradea?"

10 al día en promedio. La mayoría saltados antes de que levante la vista de mi café.

Mi setup:
Claude API - $20/mes
VPS en Alemania - $5/mes
poly_data - gratis
polymarket-cli - gratis
Polymarket/agents - gratis

$200 de semilla. Hace 27 días. $14.300 ahora.

Copytrade aquí: https://t.co/Bc70JR8QAd

271 trades. 74% tasa de acierto. Sharpe 2.47.

No lo he tocado en 27 días.

Se quedó mirando la pantalla por un largo rato.

"Esto es literalmente lo que simula nuestro equipo rojo. Excepto que tú realmente lo lanzaste"

Me escribió un email a la mañana siguiente.

"¿Alguna chance de que tomes una llamada con nuestro lead de políticas?"

Le dije que el artículo es la llamada. Léelo dos veces.

Demasiado tarde para gatekeep.

Padahal dulu banyak yang dijual saat valuasinya masih rendah. Ini breakdownnya:
Anthropic → $82.3 Miliar (165x return)
SpaceX → $15 Miliar (75x)
Solana → $5.1 Miliar (27x)
Robinhood → $4.9 Miliar (8x)
Genesis Digital → $3.5 Miliar (3x)
Cursor → $3 Miliar (15.000x)

Dari investasi awal yang "hanya" beberapa miliar, bisa meledak jadi puluhan miliar.

Pelajaran keras dari sini adalah timing penjualan aset di masa krisis bisa sangat mahal harganya.
FTX akhirnya selamat buat bayar kreditor, tapi opportunity cost-nya luar biasa besar.

Apa pendapat kalian, apakah SBF termasuk investor hebat di balik kasusnya?

news.text:
SBF Kembali, Jadi Orang Terkaya Dunia?

Hari ini, @SBF_FTX kembali muncul dan membagikan daftar investasi lamanya serta nilainya saat ini.

Masih ingat SBF dan FTX? Sam Bankman-Fried, atau lebih dikenal sebagai SBF, adalah pendiri FTX yang dulu dianggap jenius di dunia kripto.

Sebelum FTX bangkrut, ia melakukan berbagai investasi besar di sektor Web3 dan AI. Jika tidak terjerat masalah hukum, ia berpotensi menjadi salah satu orang terkaya di dunia.

Berikut nilai investasinya saat ini:

• Cursor: ~$200K → ~$3B (+1.499.900%)
• Anthropic: ~$499M → $82.3B (+16.400%)
• SpaceX: ~$200M → ~$15B (+7.400%)
• Solana: ~$189M → $5.1B (+2.600%)
• Robinhood: $612.5M → $4.9B (+700%)
• Genesis Digital: $1.17B → $3.5B (+200%)

Jika di totalkan, portfolio investasinya mencapai angka sekitar $114B. 

Menurut kamu, apakah SBF masih menahan posisi investasinya?

news.text:
This is probably the biggest news yet in software going headless, and will bring knowledge work agents to the masses. The new ChatGPT agents have access to any of the tools and data you want to work with, with complete coding and tool use available to them. 

Here's an example of a custom sales assistant agent uses Box as a knowledge source for accessing enterprise content securely to answer questions and generate new content on the fly. The workflows can obviously vastly far more complex as the agent can use any of the tools within Box available via MCP and CLI.

This precisely what agents will start to look like for knowledge work. You'll be able to spin them up in the foreground or background to help augment work. Big opportunity right now for headless platforms, and for all the new builders and designers of these agents in the enterprise.

news.text:
彭博社爆料的 Anthropic这次的事可能是今年AI安全圈最讽刺的笑话。

他们号称“太危险绝对不能公开发布”的顶级网络安全模型Mythos，
能批量发现零日漏洞，能入侵几乎所有系统。
只敢给苹果亚马逊思科这种级别的公司做封闭测试。

结果发布当天就被一个Discord小群偷偷摸进去了，安安静静用了整整两周。

没有啥惊天动地的黑客技术，就是三步。

第一步，从之前Mercor数据泄露的4TB文件里，翻出了Anthropic的API命名规则，

第二步，对着规则猜了几个可能的endpoint地址，

第三步，用群里一个第三方评估承包商的合法凭证，直接登进去了。

最绝的是这群人拿到了全世界最危险的网络武器之后，啥都没干，
就用它建了几个简单的网站，故意低调到连Anthropic的监控都没触发😂😂😂

直到他们把截图发给彭博社，全世界才知道这件事。

很多人说这是AI安全的重大事故，其实不然，我觉得被攻破的不是Mythos这个模型，是Anthropic整个外围的信任链。

模型能找遍全世界的零日漏洞，却防不住自己人把凭证随便分享，
也防不住供应链厂商把内部部署规则泄露出去，
甚至防不住别人猜一下自己的URL地址😆😆😆

这才是最扎心的地方，我们天天担心超级智能会失控毁灭世界，结果现在最前沿的AI实验室连最基础的运营安全都做不好。

就好比你造了一颗原子弹，把所有精力都花在防止它自己爆炸上，结果大门忘了锁，
被路过的小孩推门进来，拿原子弹炸了个鱼😂😂😂

更反直觉的是大家都以为拿到这个模型的人会去搞破坏，
结果他们选择了最无害的用法，
这说明至少到今天为止，最危险的不是AI本身，是管理AI的那些人。

Anthropic一直以安全第一自居，天天讲负责任的规模化，讲可控发布，
结果这次出事的恰恰就是他们最引以为傲的可控发布机制。

那么问题来了， AI的安全边界到底在哪呢？
在模型的权重里还是在人和系统的每一个缝隙里？欢迎交流呀！
news.category: OpenAI OR Anthropic OR Google DeepMind announcement

news.text:
Anthropic, géant américain de l’intelligence artificielle, cherche un directeur des ventes à Paris. Salaire brut proposé : entre 300 000 et 375 000 euros par an. Coût total pour l’entreprise : 529 616 euros. Ce qui reste dans la poche du salarié après impôts et charges : seulement 187 796 euros. L’État prélève donc plus de 341 000 euros sur ce seul poste. Et sur les revenus restants, la TVA viendra encore ponctionner jusqu’à 20 % de tout ce que ce cadre dépensera. Comment espérer attirer les meilleurs talents mondiaux de l’IA dans ces conditions ? C’est tout simplement irréaliste. Pourtant, une grande partie des Français réagira en affirmant que 187 796 euros par an, c’est déjà beaucoup trop. Pendant ce temps, aux États-Unis, Meta a proposé plus de 100 millions de dollars en package global pour débaucher un seul ingénieur d’OpenAI. Ces chiffres expliquent pourquoi la France est absente de la course à l’intelligence artificielle. Ils expliquent aussi pourquoi notre pays peine à repartir. Notre modèle fiscal et social dévore peu à peu nos chances. Il nous fait perdre toutes les batailles économiques d’aujourd’hui et de demain. 
https://t.co/6lVLEbknp3

news.text:
There are two types of genuinely smart people. The first is the one that has a balanced ego where they can admit that they've made a mistake and then they correct course while taking into account the input of the smartest people around them. The second is the type with a fat ego - is incapable of changing course when they are f***ing up -and they end up doubling down on their fumble.

I've seen several founders do this time & time again, now... Anthropic. 

So, for the past three weeks, I started arguing with my AI agents for the first time in years. In the beginning, I didn't see it, but in the last seven days, people close to me were telling me that I look more stressed and more irritable. And it turns out the problem was the cloud code harness and Enthropic running out of compute. I've learned the hard lesson the hard way, which is do not double down on a single company, no matter how shiny their ambassador programs may be. 

It's great to see more voices speak up. Matthew does an excellent analysis on the current situation. Check it out.
news.category: OpenAI OR Anthropic OR Google DeepMind announcement

news.text:
🚨🚨🚨 #NOVEDAD IOL ES EL PRIMER BRÓKER ARGENTINO QUE PERMITE COMPRAR Y VENDER ACCIONES CHATEANDO CON INTELIGENCIA ARTIFICIAL 🇦🇷🤖

🔹 La integración es con Claude, el asistente de IA de Anthropic, mediante el protocolo MCP (Model Context Protocol) → un estándar abierto que conecta herramientas externas (como una cuenta de bróker) con modelos de IA, permitiéndoles interpretar pedidos y ejecutar acciones concretas

🔹 ¿Qué significa en la práctica? Permite escribirle a Claude como si fuera un amigo y te ejecuta la operación en IOL

🔹¿Cómo? Tenés que vincular desde tu cuenta de Claude tu cuenta IOL

🔹 Lo que ya podés hacer desde el chat:
-Consultar saldos, portfolio y movimientos
-Comprar y vender acciones, bonos y cedears
-Suscribir y rescatar FCIs
-Colocar cauciones
-Poner stop loss y take profit
-Pedir la cadena de opciones con delta, gamma, theta y vega
-Consultar volatilidad implícita en tiempo real
-Pedirle análisis de mercado con visualizaciones

🔹 El combo que cambia las reglas del juego: herramientas que antes solo manejaban traders profesionales vía API, ahora están disponibles para cualquier inversor retail en una conversación

🔹 Argentina es vanguardia regional en esto. Ningún otro bróker de LATAM había lanzado algo así hasta hoy, dice IOL

news.text:
A neat thing we’ve been experimenting with: Codex workout sessions. Getting Codex to close the loop and validate its work is critical for higher complexity changes. To do that we want skills for high level workflows: “log in”, “upload file attachments and start a chat”, “grant this group access to a Workplace Agent”.

To do this reliably, we’ve been getting Codex to iterate on its own skills by planting “flags” CTF-style in the UI and ralphing Codex using automations in the app, making commits to iteratively refine the skills after self reflection on each attempt.

Capturing the flag is the win condition and from there codex optimized for reliability, wall clock time, and keeping up to the changing codebase.

Put in the reps with your agents!

news.text:
Introducing USVC - a single basket of high-growth venture capital, for everyone. 

No accreditation required, SEC-registered, and a very low $500 minimum. 

Includes OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, Sierra, Crusoe, Legora, and Vercel. As USVC adds more companies, investors will own a piece of that too. 

Liquidity typically comes when companies exit, but we’re aiming to let investors redeem up to 5% of the fund every quarter. This isn’t guaranteed, but if we can make it work, you won’t be locked up like in a traditional venture fund.

It runs on AngelList, which already supports $125 billion of investor capital. 

And I’ve joined USVC as the Chairman of its Investment Committee.
—

Go back to the 1500s, you set sail for the new world to find tons of gold - that was adventure capital. 

Early-stage technology is the modern version. It says we are going to create something new, and it’s risky. It’s daring.

But ordinary people can’t invest until it’s old, until it’s no longer interesting, until everybody has access to it. By the time a stock IPOs, most of the alpha is gone. The adventure is gone. Public market investors are literally last in line.

This problem has become farcical in the last decade. Startups are reaching trillion dollar valuations in the private markets while ordinary investors have their noses up to the glass, wondering when they’ll be let in.

Investing in private markets isn’t easy. You need feet on the ground. You need judgment built over years. Most people don’t have the patience to wait ten or twenty years for an investment to come to fruition.

But there is no more productive, harder-working way to deploy a dollar than in true venture capital.

USVC enables you to invest in venture capital in a broad, accessible, professionally-managed way, through a single basket of innovation, focused on high-growth startups, at all stages. 

It is how you bet on the future of tech: the smartest young people in the world, working insane hours, leveraged to the max, with code, hardware, capital, media, and community. Your dollar doesn’t work harder anywhere.

There is an old line - in the future, either you are telling a computer what to do, or a computer is telling you what to do. You don’t want to be on the wrong side of that transaction.

USVC lets you buy the future, but you buy it now. Then you wait, and if you are right, you get paid.

Get access here:

https://t.co/pAj1sqUsG0

news.text:
MCP SDK downloads just crossed 300 million a month. Anthropic published their production playbook for building agents that connect to real systems. The patterns, the tradeoffs, and what the numbers actually tell you 🧵

TL;DR:
→ MCP SDK downloads: 300M/month, up from 100M at start of 2026
→ Tool search cuts tool-definition tokens by 85%+ while keeping selection accuracy high
→ Programmatic tool calling reduces token usage by ~37% on complex multi-step workflows
→ Cloudflare's MCP server covers ~2,500 endpoints in ~1K tokens using just two tools
→ CIMD + Vaults handle OAuth registration and cloud token management without building a secret store

Anthropic is naturally the authority here since they co-created MCP, so the post reads with confidence. What makes it credible is that the patterns link to real implementations: Cloudflare's two-tool server, Vaults in Managed Agents, the tool search testing data. Worth bookmarking as a reference rather than a one-time read as the spec continues to evolve.

@AnthropicAI

news.text:
ChatGPT just launched Workspace Agents.

Built a Chief of Staff agent in 11 minutes. Gmail, Calendar, Slack, HubSpot. Got a daily brief on sales loops, product priorities, risks. Useful.

But the templates are baselines, not moats.

Three things most people will miss:

Connectors are table stakes. Anyone gets HubSpot + Slack + Gmail wiring on May 6th when credit-based pricing flips on.

Institutional knowledge is the moat. Our agent fleet runs on revenue agents trained on real sales and operating data. Every client engagement compounds the memory.

Self-serve and managed both win. Same playbook as the last decade of SaaS. OpenAI takes the wide market. Managed agent fleets (Palantir forward-deployed model) take the rest.

If you're building agents, stop worrying about OpenAI eating your lunch.

Worry about whether your agents are smarter this week than last.

00:00 Workspace agents for teams
02:10 Pricing and free trial
04:58 Building a custom chief brief
10:02 Future of agent fleets

news.text: Artificial intelligance with natural dumbness
news.category: OpenAI OR Anthropic OR Google DeepMind announcement

news.text:
MIT has done the unthinkable.

They built an AI that doesn't need RAG, and it has perfect memory of everything it's ever read.

It's called Recursive Language Models (RLMs).

Right now, if you want an AI to analyze a massive dataset or document, you have two bad options.

You either stuff it all into a giant context window, where the AI gets confused and suffers from "context rot."

Or you use RAG to chop it up into summaries, permanently deleting the nuance.

This paper replaces both.

Instead of forcing the AI to read a giant prompt in one pass, RLMs treat long documents as an external environment.

The AI is placed in a sandbox. The data is stored as a Python variable.

When you ask it a question, the AI doesn't just blindly try to remember the answer.

It writes code to actively search, slice, and filter the document itself.

Then, it recursively spawns smaller "sub-AIs" to read specific snippets in parallel.

It never summarizes. It never deletes data.

It preserves every single piece of original context.

The results rewrite the limits of AI memory.

It successfully handles inputs up to two orders of magnitude beyond normal context windows, scaling easily to 10 million+ tokens.

On the hardest long-context reasoning benchmarks, a standard model scored a dismal 0.04. The RLM architecture hit 58.00.

All while costing less than running a standard massive prompt.

We’ve spent the last two years burning millions in compute trying to build bigger and bigger context windows.

But the future of AI isn’t about forcing a model to swallow a giant wall of text.

It’s about teaching it how to read.

news.text:
Great question! Here's a systematic approach to cut LLM inference costs ~10x+ while scaling:

1. **Profile everything**: Track tokens/user, prompt/response lengths, model calls per feature via observability (e.g., LangSmith/Phoenix). Spot hotspots—e.g., 80% cost from one flow?

2. **Prompt engineering**: Compress prompts (remove fluff, use few-shot sparingly). Cache identical queries with semantic hash.

3. **Tier models**: Route simple tasks to smaller/faster models (e.g., Grok-3-mini vs full). Use distillation or quantization.

4. **Optimize inference**: Batch requests, enable speculative decoding/caching layers. Hybrid: LLM for complex + rules/heursitics for 70% cases.

5. **Scale smart**: Implement user quotas, RAG for knowledge (reduces params), async/offline processing.

Measure quality/latency at each step with A/B tests. Start here and iterate—costs should scale sublinearly. What’s your stack?

news.text:
> mediocre...downstream
> almost entirely free of synthetic data
TIL

> Qwen...big gap between PPL and benchmark rating

Well, less than I expected insofar as the areas they outperform on UE (stem & code) map to where they dominate benchmarks, and generation-to-generation and within model families we see PPL improving steadily as they climb the leaderboards.

Which is not to say that you're wrong wrt Joy: seems like one can't get good generalization (/lack of catastrophic forgetting) in post-training without lots of prior slop exposure, so if Joy isn't training on a lot of synthetic data we'd expect post-trained performance to lag vs. nearer-frontier labs that are.

And ofc benchmaxxing helps benchmarks -> training on every conceivable future test-set as they do is going to show up as an edge for Qwen, too, in translating base model performance to downstream evals.

Perplexity、GPTを超える精度を低コストで実現。

——検索拡張AI向けのポストトレーニング手法を論文公開。これは要チェック。

以下、解説
＝＝＝↓↓↓

【SFT + RLパイプラインの威力】
Perplexityが開発した手法は以下を同時改善：
- 検索精度の向上
- 引用品質の強化

【Qwen vs GPTの性能比較】
Qwenモデルをベースに、事実正確性でGPTモデルと同等以上の性能を達成。しかもより低コストで実現。

検索拡張生成（RAG）において、単なるファインチューニングではなくSFT（教師あり微調整）+ 強化学習の組み合わせが鍵。
